title: invokeMethod<void> discards bool from requestNotificationPermission
description: BackgroundService._requestNotificationPermission() calls invokeMethod<void>('requestNotificationPermission') but the Kotlin handler returns result.success(granted: Boolean). The bool is silently discarded. This is intentional (the comment says 'Service starts regardless of grant or deny') but the void type lies about what the channel returns. A contributor adding logic that depends on the grant outcome would not see a compile error and would be confused why the return is always null. Suggested rewrite: either change to invokeMethod<bool?> and document the discard explicitly, or omit the return value on the Kotlin side (result.success(null)).
close_reason: Kotlin now returns result.success(null) instead of result.success(bool). Channel contract is now void→void on both sides. Comments updated to reflect that grant outcome is intentionally not returned.
